text: Family and friends of a missing North Shore surfer held a candlelight vigil in his honor tonight.
About 50 people gathered on the shore fronting the surf break known as Alligator Rock.
That's where 32-year-old Kirk Passmore of Haleiwa was last seen wiping out on a huge wave nearly 36 hours ago.
We've had video of what may be the final moments of Kirk Passmore's life.
On that morning, well-known wave cinematographer Larry Haynes was filming on those big sets from the shoreline
and was rolling at the moment when the surfer took that last ride.
His father contacted me. He wanted to share his son's last wave with everyone, so here it is.
And it is incredible. Kirk Passmore on that whiteboard to the right riding a monstrous wave.
He spun around.
He took off, and he did a great takeoff.
He makes the big drop, and as he almost makes it, he hits and catches the nose a little bit.
And flies forward in front of the lip, hits and spins.
And right when he spins, the lip just impacts him hard.
Haynes says others in the water on surfboards and jet skis immediately began searching for Passmore.
You can see just in the whitewater, like two feet.
Why is his feet up? And that's telling you he's disoriented.
He probably broke an eardrum when he impacted.
And so that's the last he's seen of him.
And hours of desperate searching have turned up little.
Looking for him all over. Can't find him. Found his board.
The front half of his board was floating.
But where is the tail? Looking for the tail.
Cole finds the tail, but the tail of the leash has been pulled through.
There's so...
And you can never find him. He never came back up.
And we're still looking for him now.
